data.title: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc. prices $550 million in convertible senior notes due 2033 to repay previous notes due 2027.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, BridgeBio Pharma, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company in Palo Alto, California, specializing in genetic diseases, revealed the pricing details of their private offering. They announced the pricing of $550 million in total of 0.75% convertible senior notes due 2033. This offering was exclusive to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A of the Securities Act of 1933. Additionally, the Company allowed initial purchasers the option to buy up to an extra $82.5 million in notes. The closing of this transaction is anticipated to take place on January 21, 2026, pending the fulfillment of standard closing requirements.

data.title: ETH aims for $3,500 breakout as it currently trades at $3,309. Mixed signals from RSI and MACD create uncertainty.
data.description: The current trading price of Ethereum stands at $3,309, with technical analysis indicating a potential target of $3,500. The RSI is showing mixed signals in the neutral zone, while the bearish MACD is adding to the complexity of predicting the ETH price. This combination creates a critical juncture for Ethereum's future price movement.

data.title: BTC price could retest $105K after consolidating above $95K support, indicators signal a potential rally.
data.description: The technical analysis of Bitcoin indicates a possible price surge towards resistance levels around $105,000. The current price is consolidating above a support level of $95,000, laying the groundwork for the next upward movement. This signifies a significant potential increase in value for Bitcoin in the near term.

data.title: Jamie Dimon rules out Fed Chair role, open to Secretary of Treasury. No chance at Fed Chair, willing to be Treasury Sec if asked by President.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.'s CEO, Jamie Dimon, categorically stated that he will not consider leading the Federal Reserve in the future. However, he expressed willingness to potentially serve as the Secretary of the Treasury if offered the position by the President. Dimon firmly rejected the idea of chairing the Federal Reserve, emphasizing that there is "positively no chance" he would take on that role if approached by any U.S. President. Despite ruling out the Federal Reserve position, Dimon indicated openness to a high-ranking Cabinet position and mentioned that he would contemplate becoming the Secretary of the Treasury if requested by the President. He highlighted the importance of considering such opportunities if directly asked by the President, suggesting a willingness to take on new responsibilities. For further details, please refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
